assessment.title: A Day with Nandu - Readiness Assessment
assessment.description: Check if you're ready to learn about elephants!

questions.questionText: Which is the largest land animal?
questions.explanation: The elephant is the largest land animal on Earth.

questions.questionText: Animals that eat only plants are called:
questions.explanation: Herbivores are animals that eat only plants. Examples include elephants, cows, and deer.

questions.questionText: Which of these is NOT a body part?
questions.explanation: Water is not a body part. Trunk, tail, and legs are body parts.

questions.questionText: True or False: Some animals live in groups.
questions.explanation: True! Many animals like elephants, lions, and wolves live in groups for protection and hunting.

questions.questionText: Which unit is used to measure the weight of heavy animals?
questions.explanation: Kilograms (kg) is used to measure weight. Elephants can weigh thousands of kilograms!

questions.questionText: A baby cow is called a calf. What might a baby elephant be called?
questions.explanation: Like baby cows, baby elephants are also called calves!

questions.questionText: True or False: All animals need water to survive.
questions.explanation: True! All animals need water to survive. Elephants drink a lot of water every day.
Elephants live in forests and grasslands in Africa and Asia.

excellent.message: Excellent! You have strong background knowledge about animals. You're ready to dive deep into learning about elephants!

good.message: Good job! You know quite a bit about animals. You're ready to learn about elephants.

needsReview.message: You have some knowledge about animals. Let's quickly review some basics before learning about elephants.

needsPrep.message: Let's build some foundation knowledge about animals first. Then learning about elephants will be much easier!
